{{Quote|I have come to return you to the light.|Noab Hulis|<ref name="totjc" />}}
'''Noab Hulis''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in the [[Time/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. He sired three daughters, each of whom would go on to serve the Jedi Order themselves. Hearing of the Jedi [[Chamma]], who had become a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and gone into self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] for over a century after having felt that [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] had betrayed him, Hulis set about attempting to bring Chamma back to both the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] and the Jedi Order. Seeking out the former Jedi on [[H'ratth]], the Miraluka at first found it exceedingly difficult to get through to Chamma. Eventually Hulis relayed Chamma's own story to him, evoking century-old memories. Hulis remonstrated that Chamma had not been betrayed by the Force, but rather that he had betrayed it, so the former Jedi returned to the Order, going on to become a significant [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]].

Hulis also made connections with wealthy members of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Noble/Legends|nobility]], such as [[Barrison Draay]] and [[Alok Adasca]]. Draay in particular was of interest to Hulis, as he was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. The Miralukan paved the way for Draay's rapid progression through the Jedi ranks, as he was trained by Jedi Master [[Arca Jeth]] on [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]]. Draay also grew close to one of Hulis's daughters, [[Krynda Draay|Krynda]], who had been trained by Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]. Hulis saw both Draay and Krynda [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighted]] on Arkania by Master Jeth—the two later [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and Krynda gave birth to Hulis's grandchild, [[Lucien Draay|Lucien]]. The Great Sith War, however, took its toll on the family, as the lives of both Draay and one of Hulis's daughters were [[Death/Legends|claimed]] in the conflict.

==Biography==
===Recovering Chamma===
[[File:NoabHulisAndHisBuddies.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Hulis with Lords Adasca and Draay]]
{{Quote|You do not need that, Chamma; we have no dispute. You need only listen, and then I will take my leave.|Noab Hulis|<ref name="totjc" />}}
Noab Hulis was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="totjc" /> In [[Time/Legends|time]], he sired [[Krynda Draay's sister|two]] [[Noab Hulis's other daughter|daughters]], both of whom entered the Jedi Order,<ref name="kotor33" /> and then a third, [[Krynda Draay|Krynda]], with a [[Krynda Draay's mother|Human female]]. Krynda possessed both the [[Farsight/Legends|precognitive abilities]] of the Miraluka, and the functioning [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] of a [[Human/Legends|Human]], and was later accepted into the Jedi Order herself, training under [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]].<ref name="kotorh" />

Hulis later came to hear of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Chamma]], a former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who over a hundred years prior had journeyed to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Athiss/Legends|Athiss]] with two compatriots to confront a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sithspawn/Legends|disturbance]]. Chamma had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] during the expedition, and after destroying the Sith creature, he felt that [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] had betrayed him. As a result, Chamma went into self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] on [[H'ratth]], and there he had stayed for over a century. Hulis sought to confront Chamma and bring him back into the Jedi fold.<ref name="totjc" />

Hulis located Chamma on H'ratth, and found the former Jedi [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]]. The Miraluka waited in Chamma's living quarters for him to awaken—when he did so, Chamma confronted him. Hulis stated his intention to bring Chamma back to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]], and the former Jedi responded by taking his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], bidding Hulis leave his residence. The Miralukan obeyed, but returned the next day. Again he was rebuked by Chamma.<ref name="totjc" />

Hulis continued to visit Chamma's residence day after day until the former Jedi finally agreed to listen to what the Miralukan had to say. Hulis identified himself, and told a tale of a fallen Jedi who had become a Sith, and attracted two followers to his cause. The Sith had lured three Jedi Knights to them, in the hopes of turning them to their cause. The Jedi Knights, however, had hoped to defeat the Sith, although they lacked the requisite experience. Chamma realized that Hulis was telling ''his'' tale, in an attempt to muster century-old memories in the former Jedi. According to the Miraluka, the Sith split the Jedi Knights up, and forced them to give in to their anger. The youngest of the Knights, however, had overcome the Sith ringleader with his newfound knowledge of the dark side. As Hulis finished his story, Chamma realized that he had not been betrayed by the Force, but that it was he who had betrayed it by falling to the dark side. As a result, Chamma returned to the Jedi Order, and after training on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], he became a Jedi Master who went on to instruct the likes of [[Andur Sunrider]] in the ways of the Force.<ref name="totjc" />

===Family man===
[[File:Noab_Hulis.png|thumb|Hulis as Keeper of the [[Noetikon of Light]]]]
{{Quote|Now let's see if you can do something about that reputation for ''nepotism''. What does this make, ''three'', daughters in the Jedi Order for you?|Alok Adasca|<ref name="kotor33" />}}
In time, Hulis made friends in high places. The [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Alok Adasca|Alok]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Adasca, and the Human [[Barrison Draay|Barrison]], Lord Draay, both counted Hulis among their acquaintances. Draay in particular had demonstrated an aptitude for the Force, and trained at Master [[Arca Jeth]]'s [[Arca Jeth's praxeum|praxeum]] on [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]]. Hulis had advocated the progression of Draay through the Jedi ranks—the young Human hailed from a [[Draay family|wealthy estate]], and had brought his retainer, [[Haazen]], along to the praxeum to also be trained as a Jedi. Hulis was greatly impressed by Draay's choice, as it demonstrated that all were equal in the eyes of the Jedi. Over time, Draay grew close to Hulis's daughter, Krynda.<ref name="kotor33" />

When it came time for both Draay and Krynda to be [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighted]], Hulis attended a lavish reception held by Adasca—the Arkanian jokingly referred to the event as a "wake," as Draay would finally be leaving behind his former life of business for the path of a Jedi. Hulis sang Draay's praises, commending the young man before being led off by Adasca. Later, Hulis attended the knighting ceremony of his daughter and Draay. Master Jeth presided over the knighting, during which Haazen was overlooked for promotion. Draay and Krynda later [[Marriage/Legends|married]], although Draay would [[Battle of Toprawa (Great Sith War)|perish]] years later during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], although not before siring a son of his own, [[Lucien Draay|Lucien]].<ref name="kotor33" /> That conflict also [[Death/Legends|claimed]] the life of one of Hulis's other daughters. Krynda went on to found the [[Jedi Covenant]], watching for the return of the Sith.<ref name="kotorh" /> Although the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] of Hulis flowed in Lucien's veins, he did not inherit the ability to see into the future from his mother, as the Miralukan blood did not run strongly enough.<ref name="kotor9">[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|All who walk our path are equal.|Noab Hulis|<ref name="kotor33" />}}
Hulis was persistent, continually attempting to reason with Chamma even when threatened with weapons and continually rebuked. He was an accomplished storyteller, as they alone were able to rekindle ancient memories in Chamma's mind, convincing him to take up the path of a Jedi once more. Initially, he chose not to cover his pupil-less Miraluka eyes,<ref name="totjc" /> but opted to do so in his advanced age. With the admission of three of his daughters into the order, Hulis gathered a reputation for nepotism, although he also strongly advocated the development of other Jedi, such as Barrison Draay—despite the Human being initially older than the norm when he began his Jedi instruction.<ref name="kotor33" />

==Powers and abilities==
As a Miraluka, Hulis possessed [[Force sight]] and the ability to see into the future—skills he passed on to his daughter, Krynda.<ref name="kotorh" /> He also wielded a lightsaber, [[Color/Legends|green]] in hue.<ref name="kotor33" />

==Behind the scenes==
Noab Hulis was first mentioned in the ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', published in [[1996]] and written by [[George R. Strayton]]. Twelve years later, the character made his first appearance, in flashbacks of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] comic series issue [[Knights of the Old Republic 33|''Vindication, Part 2'']]. He was also rendered for the first time, by [[Bong Dazo]]. In the issue, Hulis is shown to cover his useless Miralukan eyes, whereas in the ''Tales of the Jedi Companion'', he is said to leave them exposed.
